object#relation @ subject

group:eng#member @ user:alice
doc:roadmap#editor @ group:eng#member
doc:roadmap#parent @ folder:planning
folder:planning#viewer @ user:bob

check(alice, edit, doc:roadmap)
walk: alice in eng · eng is editor
=> Permit

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 365px; margin-left: 454px;

div.div: Permissions are stored as relationship tuples, not roles or rules. A check walks the graph; the same index answers 'list every doc alice can edit' in one query - the reverse lookup policy engines struggle with.
